commands.review_summary:
The PR gates newly allowlisted official org handles on active reserved-handle ownership, blocks unreserved self-serve creation for those handles, updates Convex regression coverage, adds an official-publishers spec note, and adds a new npm package-lock file.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main treats any active org with handle `nvidia` as Official and lets self-serve creation proceed unless a separate reserved-handle record already blocks another user. I did not run tests because this is a read-only review, but the current-main and PR-head code paths are clear.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the remaining blocker is official-publisher production rollout proof and package-lock removal, with a MEMBER-authored security PR requiring maintainer judgment.

target.title: fix(official): require verified ownership for newly allowlisted official org handles
target.body:
## Summary

- What changed: Added reserved-owner verification and a dedicated org-level check by introducing `isReservedOwnerVerifiedOfficialOrgHandle` and `isOfficialOrgPublisher` and consulting them from `isOfficialPublisher` in `convex/lib/officialPublishers.ts`. Blocked self-serve org creation for reserved-owner-verified official handles in `createOrgPublisherForUser` unless the handle is already reserved for the actor. Exposed a `DbCtx`-friendly `getLatestActiveReservedHandle` helper in `convex/lib/reservedHandles.ts`. Added regression tests in `convex/lib/officialPublishers.test.ts` covering org-level and personal-publisher membership cases for `nvidia`, and added a spec update in `specs/official-publishers.md`.
- Why: A new allowlist entry (e.g. `nvidia`) made Official status rely solely on the mutable publisher handle, which allowed handle-squatting and unauthorized Official package publishing. The change binds Official status to a verified owner/reservation so unclaimed or attacker-created orgs cannot acquire Official privileges by registering the handle.

## Linked Issue

## Screenshots

- [x] Screenshots/recordings attached, or `N/A`

## Behavioural Proof

- Require an active `reservedHandles` record whose `rightfulOwnerUserId` owns the org before treating a newly allowlisted handle (e.g. `nvidia`) as Official, both for org publishers and for personal publishers deriving Official status via org membership.
- Block self-serve org creation for reserved-owner-verified official handles unless the actor is the rightful owner.
- Regression tests confirm: unreserved `nvidia` org → not official; reserved+rightful-owner-owned `nvidia` org → official; personal publisher member of unreserved `nvidia` org → not official; personal publisher member of reserved+rightful-owner-owned `nvidia` org → official.

- [x] Behavioural proof included, or `N/A`

## Security / Trust Impact

- [x] Security/trust impact explained

Prevents handle-squatting on newly allowlisted official handles (e.g. `nvidia`). Official status for these handles is now gated on an admin-created `reservedHandles` record binding the handle to a specific `rightfulOwnerUserId`, and self-serve org creation for those handles is blocked for non-rightful-owners.

## Data / Deploy Impact

- [x] No data/deploy impact

## Verification

- [ ] `bun run ci:static`
- [ ] Focused tests for touched behavior: `bunx vitest run convex/lib/officialPublishers.test.ts convex/publishers.test.ts --reporter verbose` — all passed
- [ ] `bun run ci:unit` or `N/A` for docs/config-only: `bun run ci:unit` passed
- [ ] Broader gate when required (`ci:types-build`, `ci:packages`, `ci:e2e-http`, `ci:playwright-smoke`, `test:pw:local-auth`, `proof:ui`): `bun run ci:types-build` passed
- [ ] Other: `bun run format:check && bun run lint` passed; `bun run ci:static` and `bun run deadcode:ci` could not fully run in this environment due to `bun audit` and `knip`/registry access issues (environment/tooling limits, not regressions)
